quality.rationale: 基于封闭FRW度规与共形时间变换的光子测地线推导，逻辑链条完整且具标准宇宙学物理背景

origin.question: Problem 19.20. For a closed $(k = 1)$ Friedmann universe in which radiation dominates for only a negligibly short fraction of the life of the universe, how many times can a photon encircle the universe from the moment of the creation of the universe to the moment of its death?## SolutionSolution 19.20. Measuring time in terms of development angle $\eta$ defined by $\mathrm {d}\eta = \mathrm {d}t / \mathbb {R}(t)$ , the Friedmann metric for a radial $(\mathrm {d}\theta = \mathrm {d}\phi = 0)$ photon becomes$$0 = \mathrm {d} s^{2} = \mathbb {R}^{2} (\eta) (- \mathrm {d} \eta^{2} + \mathrm {d} \chi^{2})$$where $\mathrm{d}\chi^2 = \mathrm{d}\mathbf{r}^2 /(1 - \mathbf{r}^2)$ is the "trigonometric" radial coordinate on the 3-sphere (see Problem 19.5). From Problem 19.18 we see that the universe lives an interval $\Delta \eta = 2\pi$ (time between two zeros of $\mathbb {R}$ ), so in this time a photon can propagate $\Delta \chi = 2\pi$ , i.e. exactly once around the universe.
origin.solution: Measuring time in terms of development angle $\eta$ defined by $\mathrm {d}\eta = \mathrm {d}t / \mathbb {R}(t)$ , the Friedmann metric for a radial $(\mathrm {d}\theta = \mathrm {d}\phi = 0)$ photon becomes$$0 = \mathrm {d} s^{2} = \mathbb {R}^{2} (\eta) (- \mathrm {d} \eta^{2} + \mathrm {d} \chi^{2})$$where $\mathrm{d}\chi^2 = \mathrm{d}\mathbf{r}^2 /(1 - \mathbf{r}^2)$ is the "trigonometric" radial coordinate on the 3-sphere (see Problem 19.5). From Problem 19.18 we see that the universe lives an interval $\Delta \eta = 2\pi$ (time between two zeros of $\mathbb {R}$ ), so in this time a photon can propagate $\Delta \chi = 2\pi$ , i.e. exactly once around the universe.
